attributes.content:
## Overview

The GitHub Repo Card card gives the workspace a structured home for gitHub repository reference with stats (stars, forks, issues), last commit, and quick actions.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

GitHub Repo Card is designed to participate in active workflows. Its state, relationships, and outputs need to remain inspectable so that agents and humans can collaborate on the same object without losing context.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Integration]]** -- GitHub Repo Card can sync with or depend on external systems through the shared integration layer.
- **[[Trigger]]** -- Changes to GitHub Repo Card can fire automation, and automation can also create or update GitHub Repo Card.
- **[[Task]]** -- GitHub Repo Card often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Project]]** -- GitHub Repo Card can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- GitHub Repo Card can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Tool Definition]]** -- Tool calls can read from or write to GitHub Repo Card when automation needs structured side effects.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create GitHub Repo Card as the structured home for gitHub repository reference with stats (stars, forks, issues), last commit, and quick actions and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates GitHub Repo Card when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** GitHub Repo Card stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: GitHub Repo Card
cardInfo.summary: GitHub repository reference with stats (stars, forks, issues), last commit, and quick actions.
